sourceMovie.title: 14 Blades
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: 14 Blades (2010) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Daniel Lee's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 14 Blades clicked for you, Dragon (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 14 Blades clicked for you, Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 14 Blades?
faq.answer: Try Dragon, Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, Hero—each shares DNA with 14 Blades while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 14 Blades?
faq.answer: 14 Blades (2010) is best described as Drama, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 14 Blades worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
